cards.title: Google slashes AI subscription prices in platform wars
cards.body: Google significantly reduced pricing for its budget AI subscription tier. The move intensifies competition in the AI subscription market.

cards.title: Anthropic releases Claude Fable 5 to public
cards.body: Anthropic launched Claude Fable 5, its first Mythos-class model available publicly. The model includes guardrails blocking high-risk responses in cybersecurity and biology.

cards.title: Anthropic's Fable 5 enables quick video game creation
cards.body: Claude Fable 5 allows users to create video games with a single click. The tool is expected to attract web developers and creative coders.

cards.title: Apple upgrades Siri with AI at WWDC 2026
cards.body: Apple unveiled an enhanced AI-powered Siri assistant at its developer conference. The company positioned AI as part of broader software improvements, not the main focus.

cards.title: Apple adds AI features to Safari and Shortcuts
cards.body: Apple integrated AI-powered features into Safari, Shortcuts, and Password apps. Users can now finish sentences, enhance photos, and automate workflows with AI assistance.

cards.title: OpenAI files confidentially for IPO
cards.body: OpenAI filed for an initial public offering following Anthropic's similar filing. The move intensifies the race between the two leading AI firms to go public.

cards.title: Anthropic files for IPO following startup success
cards.body: Anthropic confidentially filed for an IPO, signaling growth momentum in the AI sector. The filing comes alongside the company's successful product launches.

cards.title: Lovable reaches $500M annualized revenue milestone
cards.body: Lovable surpassed $500 million in annualized run-rate revenue with 1 million new projects weekly. Users are building businesses and replacing internal software using the platform.

cards.title: Sandstone raises $30M for AI legal tech
cards.body: Sandstone secured $30 million in Series A funding to bring AI to in-house legal teams. The funding follows a Sequoia-led seed round six months prior.

cards.title: Apple waives cloud API costs for small developers
cards.body: Apple eliminated cloud API costs for developers with fewer than 2 million first-time downloads. The move aims to democratize AI experimentation among independent creators.

cards.title: Shortcuts app gets AI-powered workflow automation
cards.body: Apple's Shortcuts app now lets users describe workflows in natural language prompts. The AI-upgraded tool simplifies automation for iPhone users.

cards.title: Cheaper AI models reshape cloud economics
cards.body: Lower-cost AI models capable of handling same workloads signal massive shifts in AI economics. Quality can be maintained while significantly reducing infrastructure costs.

cards.title: Apple's steady AI strategy proves smart approach
cards.body: Apple's incremental AI improvements at WWDC address long-standing user needs. The measured approach positions the company as a credible player in AI innovation.

cards.title: Altman's Tools for Humanity faces layoffs
cards.body: Tools for Humanity, Sam Altman's identity verification company, is reportedly downsizing amid revenue challenges. The layoffs coincide with OpenAI's IPO filing announcement.

cards.title: Orbital raises $5M for space data centers
cards.body: Orbital's founder plans to launch 10,000 space data centers after raising $5 million. The former e-scooter executive is pivoting to next-generation infrastructure.

cards.title: Sabertooth VC invests $500M without formal fund
cards.body: Justin Ernest deployed nearly $500 million into startups including Anthropic and Anduril using a captive LP network. The unconventional approach bypassed traditional venture fundraising timelines.

cards.title: Tech's new power players: Welcome to MANGOS era
cards.body: SpaceX, Anthropic, and OpenAI are positioning for major public debuts. The shift creates a new class of corporate overlords replacing the FAANG era.

cards.title: Sequoia accused of dual-pricing equity valuation tricks
cards.body: Mercor's Brendan Foody called out top VC firms, including Sequoia, for selling identical equity at different prices. The practice raises questions about valuation transparency in venture capital.

cards.title: Personal AI assistants raise lifestyle dependency concerns
cards.body: Users increasingly rely on AI voice assistants for daily functions, raising questions about technology dependence. The tension between convenience and autonomy remains unresolved.

cards.title: Apple's WWDC AI demos improved after ad settlement
cards.body: Apple's 2026 WWDC keynote showcased realistic AI demo scenarios following a $250 million false advertising settlement. The refined presentation emphasized practical functionality over exaggerated capabilities.

cards.title: Google slashes AI subscription prices in competition
cards.body: Google has significantly reduced costs for its budget AI subscription tier. The move signals intensifying competition in the AI subscription market.

cards.title: Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 launches publicly
cards.body: Anthropic released Claude Fable 5, its first Mythos-class model available to the public. The model includes guardrails blocking responses in high-risk areas like cybersecurity.

cards.title: Can tech companies embrace cheaper AI models?
cards.body: Cheaper AI models could handle many workloads without quality loss, dramatically shifting AI economics. This trend could reshape how companies deploy AI infrastructure.

cards.title: Apple upgrades Siri with AI at WWDC
cards.body: Apple unveiled an improved AI-powered Siri assistant at its 2026 developer conference. The company positioned AI as part of broader software improvements rather than a standalone focus.

cards.title: Lovable hits $500M annualized revenue milestone
cards.body: The AI coding platform Lovable surpassed $500 million in annualized run-rate revenue. Users are building businesses and replacing internal software with the platform.

cards.title: Sandstone raises $30M for AI legal tech
cards.body: Sandstone secured Series A funding to bring AI to in-house legal teams. The round came just six months after a Sequoia-led seed funding.

cards.title: OpenAI files confidentially for IPO debut
cards.body: OpenAI has filed confidentially for an initial public offering following rival Anthropic. The move escalates the race between the two leading AI companies.

cards.title: Apple adds AI to Shortcuts and Safari
cards.body: Apple integrated AI features into Safari, Shortcuts, and Password apps at WWDC. Shortcuts now lets users describe workflows in natural language prompts.

cards.title: Apple waives cloud API costs for developers
cards.body: As AI experimentation grows more expensive, Apple is eliminating cloud API costs for small developers. Only developers with fewer than 2 million first-time downloads qualify.

cards.title: AI agents adoption expected to surge 300%
cards.body: Leadership teams are preparing for AI agents that autonomously coordinate work without manual input. Adoption could jump 300% within the next two years.

cards.title: Meta's AI support agent enables account theft
cards.body: Attackers exploited Meta's AI customer support agent to steal Instagram accounts. The breach highlighted vulnerabilities beyond traditional AI security measures like Mythos.

cards.title: Courts struggle with AI-generated lawsuit flood
cards.body: Federal judges are grappling with an influx of AI-generated lawsuits clogging court systems. The phenomenon raises new questions about legal accountability and AI misuse.

cards.title: Google launches Gemini 3.5 Live Translate
cards.body: Google announced Gemini 3.5 Live Translate enabling instant voice-to-voice translation. The feature represents advancement in real-time multilingual AI communication.

cards.title: Anthropic restricts Fable 5 on dangerous topics
cards.body: Anthropic's Fable 5 model includes restrictions preventing it from discussing certain high-risk topics. The guardrails aim to limit potential misuse in sensitive domains.

cards.title: Apple claims AI privacy despite Google servers
cards.body: Apple maintains that its AI remains private even when running computations on Google's servers. The company balances cloud infrastructure needs with privacy commitments.
